title: Warm Dense Australian Fish Wrap
description: A briny and dense Australian dish featuring chervil, perfect for post-workout meals, wrapped in a corn husk wrap, with anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the barramundi fillets with salt, black pepper, and turmeric.
Heat the olive oil in a pan over medium heat and sear the barramundi fillets for 2 minutes on each side.
Transfer the fillets to a baking sheet and bake for 8 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix the chopped chervil, lemon juice, and minced garlic in a bowl.
Warm the corn husk wraps by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20 seconds.
Assembly the wraps by placing a cooked barramundi fillet on each wrap, topping with the chervil mixture, and serving immediately.
Let the wraps rest for 2 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
